question_text: A red filter is placed over a white light source. Which of the following best explains what is observed when viewing the light through the filter?

options.label: A
options.text: All colours of light are absorbed by the filter

options.label: B
options.text: Red light is transmitted through the filter whilst other colours are absorbed

options.label: C
options.text: Red light is reflected by the filter and all other colours are transmitted

options.label: D
options.text: The filter converts white light into red light

correct_answer: Red light is transmitted through the filter whilst other colours are absorbed
explanation: Coloured filters work by allowing light of their own colour to pass through (transmit) while blocking (absorbing) all other colours. A red filter transmits red light but absorbs blue, green, and other wavelengths of light.
